lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Redbeard Frank ye say? He be outside. Says he likes the feel of the wind on his cheeks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got any quests I could do?

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Nay, but the barkeep hears most of the news around here. Or Redbeard Frank, he's often spoken of buried treasure. Perhaps ye should be asking them for quests.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Nay, but Redbeard Frank has often spoken of buried treasure. Perhaps ye should be asking him for a quest.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Nay, but the barkeep hears most of the news around here. Perhaps ye should be asking him for a quest.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: Nay, I've nothing for ye to do. But I hear there's an old landlubber in Draynor Village who's always a-looking for a lively [lad/lass] to do him a favour.

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Nay, always ye say it wrong! Tis 'we are', not 'we be'.

lines.speaker: Jack Seagull
lines.text: I be a pirate, not a scurvy schoolmaster!

lines.speaker: Longbow Ben
lines.text: Ye be a fool, and a disgrace to piracy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll leave you two to sort it out.
